    Unlike #939 (PGLite WASM aborts on init), this bug is silent: init succeeds, migrations run, capture works — but the brain is non-functional because pgvector is missing.

    Related
    - #223 (macOS PGLite WASM crash)
    - #939 (Windows 11 PGLite WASM abort on init)
    - #1019 (Windows path validation)
title: PGLite on Windows 10 x64: gbrain init --pglite succeeds but search/think fails — pgvector extension missing from WASM binary

body:
Version: v0.41.18.0
Engine: Postgres 
Schema version: v103

SYMPTOM
gbrain dream --json runs 15/17 phases clean. extract_atoms phase fails with:

  [extract_atoms] page-discovery query failed: No database connection: 
  connect() has not been called.

Phases before and after extract_atoms (including sync, extract_facts, 
resolve_symbol_edges, consolidate, propose_takes) all use engine.executeRaw() 
successfully. Only extract_atoms fails.

ROOT CAUSE (diagnosed via source inspection)
extract-atoms.ts calls loadConfigWithEngine(engine) before discoverExtractablePages(). 
loadConfigWithEngine() calls engine.getConfig() using tagged-template SQL 
(sql`SELECT value FROM config WHERE key = ${key}`). This appears to leave the 
module-level connection singleton (db.ts) in a transient bad state where 
db.getConnection() returns "No database connection: connect() has not been called."

The catch block at line ~193 swallows the error silently, returning []. 
Phase completes with 0 atoms. Later phases recover via fresh pool connections.

IMPACT
- 0 atoms extracted despite 3,125 pages in corpus
- Error is silent — cycle exits clean, user has no visibility
- No brain damage — other phases unaffected

REPRODUCTION
1. Postgres engine (not PGLite)
2. gbrain-creator schema pack active
3. gbrain dream --json
4. Observe extract_atoms phase: page-discovery query failed error on stderr,
   0 atoms in output

EXPECTED
extract_atoms should use the same connection path as surrounding phases, 
or re-establish connection after loadConfigWithEngine() completes.
title: extract_atoms silently fails with "No database connection" on Postgres  — loadConfigWithEngine() leaves pool in bad stat

body:
## Bug Description

During a real upgrade from `gbrain 0.40.5.0` to `gbrain 0.41.18.0`, `gbrain apply-migrations --yes --non-interactive` detected that the schema was behind but then printed `All migrations up to date.` and exited successfully without applying the core schema migrations.

The follow-up command `gbrain apply-migrations --force-schema --yes --non-interactive` did apply the pending schema migrations successfully.

This is confusing during upgrades because the v0.41.18.0 changelog tells users:

> Run `gbrain apply-migrations --yes` to ensure v98/v99/v100 landed.

On this install, that exact command was insufficient; the schema stayed behind until `--force-schema` was added.

## Environment

- OS: macOS 26.5
- Runtime/package manager: Bun 1.3.14
- Install source before upgrade: `gbrain@github:garrytan/gbrain#df86ea5`
- Version before upgrade: `gbrain 0.40.5.0`
- Install source after upgrade: `gbrain@github:garrytan/gbrain#10816cb`
- Version after upgrade: `gbrain 0.41.18.0`
- DB: Supabase Postgres via PgBouncer pooler (`:6543`), with `GBRAIN_DISABLE_DIRECT_POOL=1`
- Schema before migration: v92
- Schema after `--force-schema`: v103

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start from a database whose core schema version is behind latest. In my case it was v92 while latest was v103.
2. Upgrade the GitHub-installed global package to `gbrain 0.41.18.0`.
3. Run:

   ```bash
   export GBRAIN_DISABLE_DIRECT_POOL=1
   gbrain apply-migrations --yes --non-interactive
   ```

4. Observe output.
5. Run:

   ```bash
   gbrain apply-migrations --force-schema --yes --non-interactive
   ```

## Actual Behavior

`gbrain apply-migrations --yes --non-interactive` printed:

```text
[gbrain] Prepared statements disabled (PgBouncer transaction-mode convention on port 6543). Override with GBRAIN_PREPARE=true if your pooler runs in session mode.

⚠️  Schema version 92 is behind latest 103.
   Schema migrations run automatically on next connectEngine() / initSchema().
   To run them now: gbrain init --migrate-only

All migrations up to date.
```

No core schema migrations were applied by that command.

Then `gbrain apply-migrations --force-schema --yes --non-interactive` applied the migrations:

```text
Running schema migrations from current config.version...
  Schema version 92 → 103 (10 migration(s) pending)
  [93] minions_v0_41_audit_and_budget...
  [93] ✓ minions_v0_41_audit_and_budget
  [94] take_domain_assignments...
  [94] ✓ take_domain_assignments
  [95] links_link_source_check_includes_mentions...
  [95] ✓ links_link_source_check_includes_mentions
  [96] facts_extract_conversation_session_index...
  [96] ✓ facts_extract_conversation_session_index
  [97] pages_dedup_partial_index...
  [97] ✓ pages_dedup_partial_index
  [98] gbrain_cycle_locks_last_refreshed_at...
  [98] ✓ gbrain_cycle_locks_last_refreshed_at
  [99] conversation_parser_llm_cache_table...
  [99] ✓ conversation_parser_llm_cache_table
  [101] links_link_kind_column...
  [101] ✓ links_link_kind_column
  [102] timeline_entries_source_in_dedup...
  [102] ✓ timeline_entries_source_in_dedup
  [103] migration_impact_log_and_priority_recent_idx...
  [103] ✓ migration_impact_log_and_priority_recent_idx
Applied 10 schema migration(s); now at v103.
```

## Expected Behavior

One of these should happen:

1. `gbrain apply-migrations --yes --non-interactive` applies the pending core schema migrations when it detects `Schema version X is behind latest Y`; or
2. It exits nonzero / prints a single unambiguous instruction to run `gbrain apply-migrations --force-schema --yes`; and
3. It should not print `All migrations up to date` while also saying the schema is behind.

## Why this matters

This makes upgrades easy to mis-verify. A user following the v0.41.18.0 changelog instruction can believe migrations are complete because the command ends with `All migrations up to date`, even though the schema remains behind and the new v0.41.18.0 tables/columns are not yet installed.

## Suggested Fix

- Make `apply-migrations --yes` run the same core schema migration path when stale schema is detected, or
- Change the upgrade/changelog/help text to tell users to run `apply-migrations --force-schema --yes`, and make the non-force path avoid `All migrations up to date` when schema is stale.

A regression test could seed/set schema version below latest, run `apply-migrations --yes --non-interactive`, and assert that the command either applies the pending schema migrations or exits/prints a clear actionable error without the contradictory `All migrations up to date` message.
title: apply-migrations reports “All migrations up to date” while schema is behind

body:
**What happened?**
The four `hybridSearch — reranker enabled (reorder)` tests fail on any machine whose `~/.gbrain/config.json` sets `embedding_dimensions` to something other than the test's hardcoded 1536 (e.g. text-embedding-3-small at 1280). They pass in CI only because a fresh runner has no config file. Deterministic given a config: green in CI, red on a contributor's machine.

Root cause: the test stubs the gateway at 1536, but since v0.36.3.0 `hybridSearch` resolves the embedding column from `loadConfig()`, and the resolver precedence is `cfg.embedding_dimensions > gateway dims > default`. So a real config value outranks the test's gateway dims; the stub's 1536 vector then fails the gateway dim check, the error is swallowed, search falls back to keyword-only, and the reranker never runs (rerankerFn gets 0 docs, rerank_score undefined).

Fix (test-only): isolate `GBRAIN_HOME` in `beforeAll` to a tmpdir whose config matches the stub. PR to follow.

**What did you expect?**
Pass the unit test.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. with `~/.gbrain/config.json` `embedding_dimensions` != 1536:
2. `bun test test/search/hybrid-reranker-integration.serial.test.ts` → 2 pass / 4 fail.
3.

**Environment**
- gbrain version: v0.41.14.0 (test from v0.35.0.0, broken by v0.36.3.0).
- OS:  Ubuntu 25.10
- Bun version: 1.3.11
- Database: Supabase / self-hosted Postgres

**`gbrain doctor --json` output**
```json
(paste output here)
```
title: Bug: hybrid-reranker-integration.serial.test.ts is non-hermetic (reads ~/.gbrain config)

body:
# `gbrain autopilot status` starts the daemon instead of reporting status

**Severity:** High (UX trap with safety implications)
**Repo:** garrytan/gbrain
**Affected version:** all versions; v0.41.14.0 observed, v0.41.16.0 verified unchanged at HEAD

## Summary

`gbrain autopilot status` (entered as a positional subcommand) silently **starts the autopilot daemon** rather than reporting status. The documented status check is `gbrain autopilot --status` (with the double-dash flag form). The positional form has no special handling, so it falls through to "start daemon" with `status` treated as an extra arg (likely interpreted as repo path or ignored).

This is a high-severity UX bug because:
1. Operators and AI coding assistants intuitively try `gbrain autopilot status` first (most modern CLIs accept subcommand-style status).
2. Starting the daemon has real consequences - it dispatches `sync` jobs against every source in the DB, which can trigger destructive code paths (see companion data-loss issue).
3. There is no warning that you've started the daemon. The output looks like a launch banner, which can be mistaken for status output by a casual reader.

## Reproduction

```bash
# Expectation: report current autopilot state (running? lock file? last cycle?).
gbrain autopilot status

# Actual behavior:
# Autopilot starting. Repo: <cwd>, interval: 300s
# [autopilot] Minions worker spawned (pid: -1, watchdog: 2048MB)
# [dispatch] job #2 sync (freshness: <source-id>; age=<X>min)
# ... daemon stays running ...
```

The user then has to discover that the only way to actually check status is `gbrain autopilot --status`, which is non-obvious from `gbrain autopilot --help`:

Note:  The bug only fires when `sync.repo_path` is set in config (positional `status` falls through, `parseArg(args, '--repo')` returns `undefined`, then falls back to `engine.getConfig('sync.repo_path');` if that's unset, autopilot exits with "No repo path" instead of starting).

```
Usage: gbrain autopilot

gbrain autopilot - run gbrain --help for the full command list.
```

The CLI's own help text doesn't surface `--status`. It's only documented in the source file's JSDoc:

```
src/commands/autopilot.ts lines 14-19:
 * Usage:
 *   gbrain autopilot [--repo <path>] [--interval N] [--json] [--inline]
 *   gbrain autopilot --install [--repo <path>]
 *   gbrain autopilot --uninstall
 *   gbrain autopilot --status [--json]
```

## Real-world impact

During incident investigation, I ran `gbrain autopilot status` to confirm whether autopilot was the culprit for a data-loss event. The command **started a new autopilot instance** that immediately queued a sync job against the source whose `local_path` was a real user repo. The destructive code path in `recloneIfMissing` would have fired again if the spawn-worker chain hadn't been broken on this Windows shell by an unrelated `uv_spawn ENOENT` issue.

Sequence:

```
$ gbrain autopilot status
Autopilot starting. Repo: E:/Programming/degner, interval: 300s
[autopilot] Minions worker spawned (pid: -1, watchdog: 2048MB)
[autopilot] worker spawn failed (async): ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_spawn '/c/Users/oszka/.bun/bin/gbrain' (code=ENOENT)
[dispatch] job #2 sync (freshness: gstack-code-degner-785bc61d; age=29663579min)
```

If `uv_spawn` had succeeded, the dispatched `sync` job would have run `gbrain sync --strategy code --source gstack-code-degner-785bc61d` and potentially re-triggered the data-loss path on whatever `local_path` was registered for that source.

## Proposed fix

Three options, easy → thorough:

**Minimum:** Accept positional subcommands as aliases for flags. `gbrain autopilot status` → run `--status` handler. `gbrain autopilot start` → start daemon (current behavior). `gbrain autopilot stop` → terminate daemon and clear lock. `gbrain autopilot install` / `uninstall` → corresponding flag handlers. Print a deprecation hint if positional form is used, suggesting the canonical flag form.

**Minimum+:** Like above but fail loudly.

```ts
  // After flag parsing, before lock acquisition:
  const positionals = args.filter(a => !a.startsWith('--') && /* not a flag value */);
  if (positionals.length > 0) {
    console.error(`Unknown subcommand: ${positionals[0]}. Did you mean --${positionals[0]}?`);
    process.exit(2);
  }
```

**Better:** Update `gbrain autopilot --help` so the existing flag form is discoverable. Currently the help block is one line of "run gbrain --help for the full command list" - surface the JSDoc's actual usage block at runtime.

**Best:** Both of the above PLUS require explicit consent before starting the daemon. `gbrain autopilot` alone (no args) should print state + the start command, not just start. Use `gbrain autopilot start` or `gbrain autopilot --daemon` for the actual launch. This shifts the safety burden off the user.

## Cross-references

- **Related: #578** - "`gbrain sync --help` fires a live sync instead of printing help (CLI_ONLY commands without per-command help-handling fall through to live execution)". Same class of UX trap: gbrain's CLI dispatcher falls through to "live execution" when it receives an unexpected positional/flag combo. Different command (`sync` vs `autopilot`), same root cause. A central fix (require recognized subcommand or print help) would close both.
- Critical data-loss issue (companion): `recloneIfMissing` rm-rfs user-managed source paths - this trap triggered the bug when I inadvertently re-spawned autopilot while diagnosing the incident.

> Note: PR #634 fixed the `--help/-h` surface of #578 by adding `CLI_ONLY_SELF_HELP` allowlist + generic help short-circuit in `src/cli.ts:111-121`. #1525 is the unknown-positional surface of the same family, not covered by #634 - `gbrain autopilot status` is `argv = ['status']`, not `argv = ['--help']`, so it bypasses the help short-circuit and reaches `runAutopilot()` which only branches on flags.
title: UX trap: `gbrain autopilot status` starts the daemon instead of reporting status

body:
# IngestionEvent provenance fields (source_id, source_kind, source_uri) dropped by ingest_capture handler before page write

## Summary

`ingest_capture` validates `IngestionEvent` payloads that include `source_id`, `source_kind`, and `source_uri`, but the handler does not thread those fields into `importFromContent` when it writes the page. The result is that the documented public ingestion path can accept a valid event, write a page, and silently lose the event's source isolation/provenance fields.

## Reproduction

- gbrain version: `0.41.14.0`
- Engine: PGLite
- Setup: local install, ZeroEntropy embeddings
- Steps:
  1. Submit an `IngestionEvent` through the public `ingest_capture` handler with `source_id`, `source_kind`, and `source_uri` all populated.
  2. Let the handler validate the event and write the page.
  3. Inspect the resulting page row.

The symptom is that validation passes and the page is written, but the page row is not written under the submitted `source_id`, and the `source_kind` / `source_uri` provenance fields are not persisted from the event.

## Expected vs Actual

Expected: the page has `source_id`, `source_kind`, and `source_uri` matching the submitted `IngestionEvent`.

Actual: those provenance fields are not carried through by the `ingest_capture` write path.

## Evidence

We verified the contract works end-to-end when `source_id`, `source_kind`, and `source_uri` are threaded manually via direct `importFromContent` calls. Play B documents this in detail at `https://github.com/TTJC24/gbrain-eval` (private repo, can share relevant excerpts if useful). Specifically:

- Bypassing `ingest_capture` and calling `importFromContent` directly with the provenance fields threaded: `source_id` / `source_kind` / `source_uri` all persist correctly, isolation tests pass, idempotency works.
- Using `ingest_capture` as documented: validation passes, page is written, but provenance fields are dropped before the page write.

The direct write shape used in Play B was:

```ts
await importFromContent(engine, slug, event.content, {
  sourceId: SOURCE_ID,
  noEmbed: true,
  source_kind: event.source_kind,
  source_uri: event.source_uri,
  ingested_via: 'ingestion-source',
});
```

With that direct threading, a dedicated `m365-calendar` source preserved provenance, citations, idempotency, and explicit source isolation across 21 imported calendar event pages.

## Code location

In `src/core/ingestion/types.ts`, `IngestionEvent` declares the fields as required provenance:

- lines 70-84: event carries provenance and declares `source_id`, `source_kind`, `source_uri`
- lines 262-270: `validateIngestionEvent` requires those three fields as non-empty strings

In `src/core/import-file.ts`, `importFromContent` already supports provenance write-through:

- lines 240-252: options include `source_kind`, `source_uri`, `ingested_via`
- lines 625-630: those options are passed to `tx.putPage`

In `src/core/minions/handlers/ingest-capture.ts`, the fields are validated but not passed to `importFromContent`:

- lines 56-66: event is loaded and validated with `validateIngestionEvent`
- line 116: `importFromContent(engine, slug, event.content, { noEmbed })`
- lines 122-124: `source_kind` and `source_uri` are returned in the job result, but not persisted on the page row

## Suggested fix shape

The `ingest_capture` handler's `importFromContent` call should thread the validated event provenance into the write call:

- route the page write under `event.source_id`
- persist `event.source_kind`
- persist `event.source_uri`
- stamp an appropriate `ingested_via` value for this handler path

I am intentionally describing the shape rather than attaching a patch because the right `ingested_via` taxonomy and trust-gate path may be a maintainer preference.

## Context

We're evaluating gbrain as the foundation for a company-brain platform and are porting existing operational connectors (M365, Acumatica, Pipedrive) to the `IngestionSource` contract. This bug matters because provenance drives citation, federation, and source isolation. The underlying architecture looks sound; the gap appears to be this handler-to-write handoff.

## Question for the maintainer

1. Is this by design? If the public path is intentionally not the provenance-preserving path, is there a doc pointer to the correct path so connector authors can use it?
2. If it is a bug, would you accept a PR? Happy to send one with tests if the fix shape above is on the right track.
title: IngestionEvent provenance fields (source_id, source_kind, source_uri) dropped by ingest_capture handler before page write

body:
## Bug

The `since` / `until` parameters on the `query` operation are documented as filtering on `effective_date`:

> `since`: v0.29.1 — filter to pages whose effective_date is >= this.
> `until`: v0.29.1 — filter to effective_date <= this.

But the actual SQL in `postgres-engine.ts` (lines 1401, 1406, 1554, 1559, 1676, 1681) filters on `COALESCE(p.updated_at, p.created_at)`:

```sql
AND COALESCE(p.updated_at, p.created_at) > $N::timestamptz
AND COALESCE(p.updated_at, p.created_at) < $N::timestamptz
```

## Impact

For pages ingested via `sync` or `import`, `updated_at` reflects **import time**, not the content's actual date. Email pages with filenames like `2026-05-22_$

In practice, `query --since 2026-05-18 --until 2026-05-25` returns **zero results** even though dozens of pages have `effective_date` within that range. The $

## Reproduction

1. Import a markdown directory with date-prefixed filenames
2. Verify `effective_date` is populated: `SELECT effective_date, slug FROM pages WHERE effective_date IS NOT NULL LIMIT 5;`
3. Run `gbrain query "anything" --since 2026-05-01 --until 2026-05-31` — returns empty
4. Run `gbrain query "anything"` (no date filter) — returns pages with `effective_date` in that range

## Fix

Change all 6 occurrences in `postgres-engine.ts` from:
```sql
COALESCE(p.updated_at, p.created_at)
```
to:
```sql
COALESCE(p.effective_date, p.updated_at, p.created_at)
```

This respects `effective_date` when set (filename-derived, frontmatter, etc.) and falls back to `updated_at` / `created_at` for pages without one.

The same bug likely exists in `pglite-engine.ts` (same pattern at lines ~1398–1728).

## Environment
- gbrain v0.41.14.0
- Postgres engine with pgvector
- 1,416 pages (1,227 emails with filename-derived effective_date, 189 concepts)
title: query: since/until filters on updated_at instead of effective_date

body:
## Summary

The `chat()` function in `src/core/ai/gateway.ts` normalizes AI SDK v6 `generateText` results into internal `ChatBlock[]`. It processes `result.content` typed parts (text, tool-call), but skips `type: "reasoning"` parts and does not check `result.reasoningText` in the fallback path. When a reasoning model (e.g. DeepSeek with thinking/reasoning enabled) returns content primarily through reasoning channels, the blocks array remains empty and the caller receives an empty text response.

## Reproduction

1. Configure a brain with a DeepSeek reasoning model that returns `reasoning_content`:
   ```
   chat_model: deepseek:deepseek-v4-flash
   ```
2. Run a cycle phase that calls `gateway.chat()` (e.g. proposal, significance judge)
3. The generated text is empty, causing downstream failures.

## Root Cause

The response normalization at line 2408 only handles:
- `part.type === "text"` — text content
- `part.type === "tool-call"` — tool invocation

AI SDK v6 also returns `type: "reasoning"` parts (the `ReasoningPart` type) for reasoning-model responses. The flat `result.text` fallback similarly ignores `result.reasoningText`.

## Fix

- In the `result.content` branch: treat `type: "reasoning"` parts as text blocks
- In the flat fallback: when `result.text` is empty, check `result.reasoningText` as an alternative source

See PR # for the implementation.
title: fix(gateway): handle reasoning model output falling through response normalization

body:
## Summary

`diagnoseEmbedding()` in `src/core/ai/gateway.ts` returns `user_provided_model_unset` for any recipe where `touchpoints.embedding.models` is an empty array and `user_provided_models === true`, even when the user has already configured an explicit model via `embedding_model`.

## Reproduction

1. Configure a brain with `llama-server` as the embedding provider:
   ```
   embedding_model: llama-server:text-embedding-qwen3-embedding-0.6b
   ```
2. Run `gbrain doctor` or any autopilot cycle that triggers embed preflight:
   ```
   gbrain doctor --remediate --yes
   ```
3. The preflight returns `user_provided_model_unset` despite the model being fully specified.

## Root Cause

`diagnoseEmbedding()` resolves `embedding_model` via `resolveRecipe()`, which parses the model string into `{ providerId, modelId }`. But the subsequent guard at line 670 only checks `tp.models.length === 0` — it never verifies that `parsed.modelId` (the user-provided model id) is actually populated.

```typescript
// Current — incomplete guard
if (
  Array.isArray(tp.models) &&
  tp.models.length === 0 &&
  (recipe.id === "litellm" || isUserProvided)
) {
  return { ok: false, reason: "user_provided_model_unset", ... };
}
```

## Fix

Add `!parsed.modelId` to the condition. If the model string carries a non-empty model id after the colon, the user has provided a model — the preflight should pass.

See PR # for the implementation.
title: fix(gateway): diagnoseEmbedding rejects user-provided models for user_provided_models recipes (llama-server)

body:
## Summary

On a healthy Postgres setup (daemon `gbrain serve --http` working fine), invoking `gbrain dream` from CLI causes every batch insert during `extract.links_fs` and `extract.timeline_fs` to fail with `No database connection: connect() has not been called`. The phase nonetheless reports `status: ok` (because successful-count is 0 and no phase-level error is raised), so production cron silently no-ops indefinitely.

## Reproduction

Fork-side gbrain 0.41.14.0, macOS 26.3, Bun 1.3.x, Postgres 16 at `127.0.0.1:5432`, ~3100 markdown pages under `~/brain/`. `~/.gbrain/config.json`:

```json
{
  "engine": "postgres",
  "database_url": "postgresql://USER@127.0.0.1:5432/gbrain",
  "embedding_model": "google:gemini-embedding-001",
  "embedding_dimensions": 1536
}
```

The same Postgres process is used by `gbrain serve --http` (running concurrently and healthy — `/health` returns ok, `put_page` / `query` etc. all work via MCP).

```bash
gbrain dream --json --dir ~/brain
```

Output excerpt (3000+ batches all fail):

```
[cycle.extract] start
[extract.links_fs] start
[extract.links_fs] connection blip, retrying 100 rows in 500ms (No database connection: connect() has not been called. Fix: Run gbrain init --supabase or gbrain init --url <connection_string>)
  batch error (100 link rows lost): No database connection: connect() has not been called. Fix: Run gbrain init --supabase or gbrain init --url <connection_string>
[extract.links_fs] 30/3099 (0%)
[continues for all 3099 pages, every batch fails the same way]
...
[extract.links_fs] 3099/3099 (100%) done
[extract.timeline_fs] start
[same failure pattern]
[extract.timeline_fs] 3099/3099 (100%) done
[cycle.extract] done
```

Final JSON report from `gbrain dream --json`:

```json
{
  "status": "partial",
  "phases": [
    ...
    {
      "phase": "extract",
      "status": "ok",
      "summary": "0 link(s), 0 timeline entries (incremental: 0 slugs)"
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```

Note: phase status is `ok` despite 100% of batches failing. The successful-count happens to equal the to-process-count (both 0 after the retries exhaust), so the phase summary reports a "clean noop" when in fact every batch failed.

## What's puzzling

The `[dream] WARNING: could not connect to DB` line from `src/cli.ts:1082-1086` does **NOT** fire — `connectEngine()` returns without throwing. But the engine instance reaching `extract.links_fs` clearly isn't connected:

```ts
// src/cli.ts:1068-1093 (dream command)
let eng: BrainEngine | null = null;
try {
  eng = await connectEngine();       // ← succeeds, no warning
} catch (err) {
  process.stderr.write(`[dream] WARNING: could not connect to DB ...`);
}
await runDream(eng, args);            // ← engine passed in

// Inside the cycle later:
// src/commands/extract.ts:711-712
created += await withRetry(
  () => engine.addLinksBatch(snapshot),   // ← throws "No database connection: connect() has not been called"
  ...
);
```

Either:
- The engine instance reaching `addLinksBatch` is NOT the one cli.ts connected (lifecycle drift / singleton resolution issue across cycle phases), OR
- The connection got reset / disconnected between phases, OR  
- `engine.connect()` succeeds but doesn't actually open a working Postgres connection (silent lazy init that defers connection until first query, and that deferred connect fails)

## Production impact

The fork has been running the nightly dream-cycle since v0.40.x via a `launchd` plist that fires `gbrain dream --json --dir ~/brain` at 03:11 daily. Reports archived to `~/brain/.agent/dream-cycles/<ISO>.json`. **Every report from 2026-05-19 to 2026-05-26 (8 days) shows `partial` status with `0 link, 0 timeline entries`** — i.e. extract has been silently broken in production for at least 8 days, possibly since the §6.28 (2026-05-17) cutover when the brain transitioned to DB-canonical mode (no more markdown write-through).

This is a particularly bad failure mode because:
- Cron exits 0 (status `partial` with all phases `ok` / `warn` / `skipped`)
- launchd doesn't flag it
- The archived JSON report looks normal in skim — only the bare "0 links" tells you anything
- batch errors only surface on stderr (manual run reveals them; cron silently discards them)

## Suggestion (priority order)

1. **Phase-level error escalation** (highest priority): when a batch operation throws "No database connection", the cycle phase should bubble that error and report phase `status: failed`, not `ok`. Hiding production failure behind an `ok` summary is the worst failure mode.

2. **Engine instance audit**: trace why `engine.addLinksBatch` sees an unconnected engine when `cli.ts` clearly called `connectEngine()` successfully. Look for singleton vs per-phase engine resolution; check if `runDream` or its subroutines re-instantiate an engine that bypasses the connect call.

3. **Health probe in `runDream`**: cheap sanity check at the top — `await engine.executeRaw('SELECT 1')` — and if it fails, throw before the cycle starts so the "could not connect" path actually fires. The current `try { connectEngine() } catch` only catches errors that happen during `connectEngine`, not connection-state issues that surface later.

## Workaround (current)

None known. `--dry-run` succeeds because no writes happen, but no actual links/timeline are extracted. Production fork is currently relying on the daemon (`gbrain serve --http`) for all DB writes via MCP `put_page` etc. (those work fine, daemon has its own connection lifecycle).

## Related notes

- Fork's `~/.gbrain/config.json` is the standard shape per docs.
- No env shadow of `DATABASE_URL` — config-only.
- M3 cutover (2026-05-10) retired an in-process embed-shim; this issue is independent of that (the bug is in core DB engine lifecycle, not embedding).
- `gbrain init` works fine; this happens AFTER successful init.
title: dream-cycle extract.links_fs/timeline_fs phases silently fail with "No database connection" on Postgres engine (v0.41.14.0)
doctor: pgvector and jsonb_integrity checks throw false warnings on PGLite

body:
## Situation

I have an existing source in my brain (created ~v0.36, never refreshed) that holds markdown-only content imported from a repo path. I'd like to turn on semantic code search (`gbrain code-def` / `code-refs` / `code-callers`) for that repo via gstack's `/sync-gbrain --full`. The orchestrator tries to register a fresh `gstack-code-<slug>-<hash>` source at the same path and gets rejected:

```
source registration failed: path "/path/to/repo" overlaps with existing
source "<old-id>" at "/path/to/repo". Overlapping sources are not allowed.
```

## What I tried

- **`gbrain sources archive <id>`** — `softDeleteSource` (`src/core/destructive-guard.ts:187`) flips `archived=true` + `federated=false` but leaves `local_path` populated. The overlap check at `addSource` (`src/core/sources-ops.ts:295`) doesn't filter archived rows, so the path stays claimed.
- **`gbrain sources detach`** — only removes the worktree `.gbrain-source` dotfile. Doesn't touch the source row.
- **`gbrain sources rename`** — display-name only; `id` and `local_path` are immutable from the CLI.

No source-merge, source-rebase-onto-new-strategy, or update-local-path command appears to exist.

The only paths I see that would unblock:

1. `gbrain sources purge <id> --confirm-destructive` (hard-deletes, no archive required per `src/commands/sources.ts:413`). Loses the existing semantic-typed markdown pages permanently and may fail if any `oauth_clients.source_id` references the old id (`ON DELETE RESTRICT` per `schema.sql:600`).
2. Raw SQL to set `local_path = NULL` on the old source (non-canonical).

## Question

What's the canonical way to migrate an existing markdown-strategy source to a code-strategy source at the same path, without losing the existing pages? Or is this a "purge + re-import" situation where the old markdown content is expected to be sacrificial?

Happy to PR a `gbrain sources reclaim-path <id>` or `gbrain sources change-strategy <id>` if that's the right shape and isn't already in flight.

## Environment

- gbrain v0.41.11.1, postgres engine, local-stdio MCP mode
title: Canonical way to migrate a markdown-strategy source to a code-strategy source at the same path?

body:
# Cycle extract phase still writes links/timeline to `default` source on federated brains (follow-up to #1204)

## Summary

#1204 fixed the **CLI** `gbrain extract` source-awareness by adding `--source-id` (v0.37.7.0). But the **automated dream/autopilot cycle** still hits the same root cause on a federated brain: every cycle logs `Links: created 0 from N pages` / `Timeline: created 0 entries from N pages` and persists nothing, because the cycle's extract phase never threads a `sourceId` and falls back to the literal `'default'` source.

Two interacting defects, both still present on **v0.41.2.0**:

1. **`runPhaseExtract` doesn't thread `sourceId`.** It calls `runExtractCore({ mode, dir, slugs })` with no source. `ExtractOpts` has no `sourceId` field, so the full-walk extractors (`extractLinksFromDir`/`extractTimelineFromDir`) never set `from_source_id/to_source_id`, and `addLinksBatch` maps missing → literal `'default'`, then INNER-JOINs `pages ON (slug, source_id='default')`. On a brain whose content lives in a non-`default` source, every row is dropped by the JOIN → 0 inserted, no error. (Same mechanism as #1204 Bug 1, just reached via the cycle instead of the CLI.)

2. **`noExtract: willRunExtractPhase` disables the *working* path.** `runPhaseSync` resolves the correct `sourceId` (`resolveSourceForDir`) and `performSync` would run the source-aware inline hooks (`extractLinksForSlugs`/`extractTimelineForSlugs`, which #1204 confirmed are correct). But sync passes `noExtract: willRunExtractPhase` to avoid duplicate work — so when the cycle's extract phase is scheduled (the default, `ALL_PHASES`), sync **skips its source-correct inline extraction** and defers to the broken full-walk phase. Net: the cycle turns off the good path and keeps the broken one.

## Environment

- gbrain **v0.41.2.0**, Supabase Postgres engine
- Federated brain, 3 sources: `default` (0–10 pages, isolated), `sos` (~32.7k pages, repo-backed, the live vault), `msft` (~2.8k pages)
- Symptom in `~/.gbrain/autopilot.log`, every cycle:
  ```
  Links: created 0 from 13671 pages
  Timeline: created 0 entries from 13671 pages
  ```

## Repro

On a federated brain whose content is in a non-`default` source, run a full dream cycle (or let autopilot run). The extract phase reports `created 0`. Meanwhile the source-scoped CLI path works:

```
$ gbrain extract all --source db --source-id sos --dry-run
{ "links_created": 91, "timeline_entries_created": 376, "pages_processed": 32701 }
```

vs. the cycle's full-walk phase on the same content: `created 0`.

## Relevant code (v0.41.2.0)

- `src/core/cycle.ts:810` — `runPhaseExtract` calls `runExtractCore(engine, { mode: 'all', dir: brainDir, slugs: changedSlugs })`, no `sourceId`.
- `src/commands/extract.ts:302-318` — `ExtractOpts` has no `sourceId` field; the full-walk path (`extractLinksFromDir`/`extractTimelineFromDir`) can't scope.
- `src/core/postgres-engine.ts:2064-2077` — `addLinksBatch` maps missing `from_source_id/to_source_id` → `'default'`, INNER-JOINs `pages ON (slug, source_id)`.
- `src/core/cycle.ts:745-752` — `runPhaseSync` resolves the correct `sourceId` but passes `noExtract: willRunExtractPhase`, disabling the source-aware inline hooks when the (broken) extract phase will run.
- `src/core/cycle.ts:1371` — `willRunExtractPhase = phases.includes('extract')`.
- Working, source-aware hooks already exist: `src/commands/extract.ts:719` (`extractLinksForSlugs`) / `:748` (`extractTimelineForSlugs`), used by `src/commands/sync.ts:1104-1109`.

## Suggested fix

Thread the source through the cycle extract path, mirroring what `runPhaseSync` already does:

1. Add `sourceId?: string` to `ExtractOpts`; have `runExtractCore` pass it to the full-walk extractors and on to `addLinksBatch` (the incremental `extractForSlugs`/`extractLinksForSlugs` path already accepts `{ sourceId }`).
2. In `runPhaseExtract`, resolve `sourceId = await resolveSourceForDir(engine, brainDir)` and pass it down.

Alternatively (or additionally): when the cycle can't resolve a single source for `brainDir`, fall back to the source-aware sync-inline extraction (i.e., don't set `noExtract` for the broken full-walk path) so federated brains aren't silently left at 0 coverage.

## Impact

On federated brains the nightly cycle contributes nothing to graph/timeline coverage; coverage is maintained only by the per-source sync hook on *changed* pages. Already-synced pages that were never individually extracted stay uncovered, and there's no automated path to close the gap (the manual workaround is `gbrain extract all --source db --source-id <id>`, the #1204 fix).
title: Cycle extract phase still writes links/timeline to `default` source on federated brains (follow-up to #1204)

body:
﻿## Bug Description

Same `Aborted(). Build with -sASSERTIONS for more info.` PGLite WASM crash as #223, but on **Windows 11 ARM64** — not macOS. The user-facing wrapper in `gbrain` currently attributes this only to the macOS 26.3 bug; the underlying failure mode appears to be cross-OS on **arm64**.

## Environment

- OS: Microsoft Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200, **ARM 64-bit Processor**
- Bun: 1.3.14
- gbrain: 0.40.8.1 (installed from `github:garrytan/gbrain#af5ee1e` via `bun install -g`)
- `@electric-sql/pglite`: 0.4.3 (note: #223 is on 0.4.4 — so the regression is not pinned to a single PGLite version)
- Engine: pglite (default), brain at `~/.gbrain/brain.pglite`

## Steps to reproduce

Any PGLite-touching command fails. Examples that hit it in my session:

```
gbrain config set autopilot.nightly_quality_probe.enabled true
gbrain query "test"
```

Both crash with:

```
PGLite failed to initialize its WASM runtime.
  This is most commonly the macOS 26.3 WASM bug: https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain/issues/223
  Run `gbrain doctor` for a full diagnosis.
  Original error: Aborted(). Build with -sASSERTIONS for more info.
```

`gbrain doctor --fast` works because it deliberately skips DB checks; `gbrain doctor` (full) does not.

## What still works

- `gbrain --version`
- `gbrain doctor --fast`
- `gbrain routing-eval` (pure-file analysis, no DB)
- `gbrain apply-migrations --yes` reports "All migrations up to date" — though I am unsure whether that codepath actually opens PGLite or just inspects the migrations dir.

## Workaround

Editing `~/.gbrain/config.json` directly (e.g. to enable `autopilot.nightly_quality_probe.enabled`) avoids the crash since `loadConfig()` reads the JSON file before any PGLite boot. This is a workaround for `config set` only; data-plane commands (`query`, `serve`, `export`, anything that reads/writes pages) still cannot run.

## Analysis / suggestion

- #223 reports the same WASM `Aborted()` on **macOS 26.3 arm64**. This is **Windows 11 arm64**. Common thread: **arm64 + PGLite WASM**.
- PGLite versions differ (0.4.4 in #223, 0.4.3 here) — so pinning PGLite alone is unlikely to fix both.
- The error wrapper string ("This is most commonly the macOS 26.3 WASM bug") should probably broaden once this is confirmed, so Windows arm64 users do not get pointed at a macOS-only issue.

Happy to gather more diagnostics (e.g. a `-sASSERTIONS`-enabled PGLite build, full Node/Bun stack with `--inspect`) if useful.
title: PGLite WASM crash on Windows 11 ARM64 (same Aborted() as #223 on macOS arm64)

body:
## Summary

During autopilot cycle execution, the lint phase creates a second PostgresEngine and shares the module-level db.sql singleton via engine.connect({}) (no poolSize). When the phase completes, engine.disconnect() in the finally block destroys the shared pool, rendering the worker unable to operate. All subsequent phases fail, and the worker crashes with an unhandled rejection.

## Impact

- All 9 sources never completed a full cycle
- Brain score stuck at 51 (links 3/25, timeline 2/15, orphans 1/15)
- Worker crash-loops: 51 crash records, max 5 restarts before autopilot gives up

## Root Cause

src/commands/lint.ts:298-319, resolveLintContentSanity():

```typescript
const engine = await createEngine({...});
try {
    await engine.connect({});  // no poolSize -> module singleton path
    const lifted = await loadConfigWithEngine(engine, base);
} finally {
    await engine.disconnect(); // -> db.disconnect() -> sql.end(); sql = null
}
```

engine.connect({}) without poolSize routes through the module-level db.sql singleton. The subsequent engine.disconnect() in the finally block kills the shared pool for ALL callers in the process, including the MinionWorker.

## Diagnosis

Instrumented db.disconnect(), PostgresEngine.disconnect(), and getConnection() with stack traces. The stack trace confirms the disconnect originates from resolveLintContentSanity -> lint.ts:319 -> runLintCore -> runPhaseLint -> runCycle.

## Fix

Pass poolSize: 2 so the lint engine gets its own instance-level pool that does not share state with the module singleton:

```typescript
await engine.connect({ poolSize: 2 });
```

## Related

The /No database connection/i pattern in retry-matcher.ts (added v0.41.2.1) marks this terminal error as retryable, producing misleading connection blip log messages and wasting a 500ms retry. Since the error means the module-level singleton is null, retrying without explicit reconnect is guaranteed to fail.
title: Bug: lint phase engine.disconnect() kills worker shared DB pool, causing crash-loop

body:
**Followup to #1342 (closed by automation when PR #1405 merged; PR body explicitly said "leave #1342 open").**

Refiling because [my comment on the closed issue](https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain/issues/1342#issuecomment-4539977640) hasn't surfaced in triage. Reproduced cleanly on **v0.41.14.0** (post #1405 + #1456).

## Sharp new finding

The 4 `[gbrain phase] sync.<phase>` stderr breadcrumbs added in #1405 (`resolve_repo`, `load_active_pack`, `validate_repo_state`, `detect_head`) **never print** when the bug fires.

Those breadcrumbs live inside `performSyncInner`. Their absence means **the hang is happening before `performSyncInner` is reached** — i.e., somewhere in `runSync` setup, engine bootstrap, or the CLI dispatch path. PR #1456 rewired the source-resolver tier inside `runSync`, but the hang still reproduces on top of that PR.

## Repro

```bash
gbrain --version
# gbrain 0.41.14.0

rm -f ~/.gbrain/brain.pglite/.gbrain-lock/lock   # clean state, no existing lock

cd "$VAULT_DIR"
gtimeout 60 gbrain sync --repo . --skip-failed --dry-run --no-pull \
  > /tmp/sync.out 2> /tmp/sync.err
```

## Result

- 98% CPU, single-threaded JS loop, no I/O wait in `sample` output
- `gtimeout 60` SIGTERM is ignored; only SIGKILL stops it
- After SIGKILL, stale `.gbrain-lock/lock` left behind pointing to dead PID
- **stderr is 0 bytes** — no `[gbrain phase]` breadcrumbs, no errors, nothing
- stdout is 0 bytes
- Reproduces with `--dry-run --no-pull` so it is not git pull, embedding, or write-path

## Suggested next breadcrumbs

To localize, two additional breadcrumbs in `runSync()` (before it calls `performSyncInner`) would help:
1. One right after CLI arg parse / `resolveSourceId` returns
2. One right before `performSyncInner(...)`

If neither prints, the hang is in `resolveSourceId` or earlier engine bootstrap. If only #1 prints, the hang is between the two.

## Process-leak side effect

Each SIGKILL'd hang leaves a bun process at 99% CPU that survives past the parent's exit. I had one running for 134 minutes from an earlier session before noticing it. PR #1405's disconnect lifecycle fixes don't catch this path because `performSyncInner` was never entered (its teardown handlers don't apply).

## Environment (unchanged from #1342)

- gbrain 0.41.14.0, bun-link install from `~/code/gbrain`
- PGLite engine at `~/.gbrain/brain.pglite`, schema v92 (then post-migration v95+)
- Embed: `ollama:nomic-embed-text` (768d)
- macOS 15.5, Apple Silicon
- Single source (`default`, 439 pages)

## Workaround in use

`gbrain call sync_brain '...'` works when `gbrain serve` is running (executes via in-process MCP tool dispatch, bypasses the broken `runSync` startup path). When serve is down, `gbrain call` hits the same hang because it falls back to bootstrapping its own engine through the same code path.

The launchd-scheduled CLI sync is disabled until a fix lands.
title: gbrain sync hangs before performSyncInner — breadcrumbs from #1405 never print (followup to #1342, persists in v0.41.14.0)

body:
### Summary

`gbrain extract links` silently creates 0 edges from an Obsidian-style vault export because the hardcoded `DIR_PATTERN` regex in `src/core/link-extraction.ts` only matches gbrain's own slug conventions. Operators ingesting any non-canonical directory layout (Obsidian vaults, Notion exports, custom topic taxonomies) get 0% graph coverage with no error and no warning.

### Where

```ts
// src/core/link-extraction.ts
const DIR_PATTERN = '(?:people|companies|meetings|concepts|deal|civic|project|projects|source|media|yc|tech|finance|personal|openclaw|entities)';
```

…and the three regex variants below it all bake `DIR_PATTERN/` into the link-target requirement.

### Impact

For an Obsidian export with directories like `books/`, `documentation/`, `guides/`, `ideas/`, `nutrition/`, `travel/`, `writing-dump/`, `archive/`, none of the wikilinks resolve. The extractor reports `created: 0 from N pages` with no clue what's wrong. Operators have to grep the source to discover the hardcoded whitelist; the docs don't mention it.

Even when the directory DOES match (e.g. `projects/` is in the whitelist), Obsidian's *title-form* wikilinks (`[[Yomumichi Documentation]]`) fail because the regex requires `[[directory/slug-path]]`. Obsidian users never write that form — Obsidian resolves by title via its own fuzzy index, and exports preserve title-form.

### Repro

```bash
# Pages exist:
gbrain sources list
# → some-source  321 pages
gbrain get projects/project-list/yomumichi/yomumichi
# → full content with [[Yomumichi Documentation]], [[Project List.base]], [[Notes on X]]

gbrain extract links --source db
# Links: created 0 from 321 pages
gbrain get-links projects/project-list/yomumichi/yomumichi
# → []
```

### Proposed fix

1. **Make `DIR_PATTERN` configurable** via `gbrain config set link_extraction_dirs "books,documentation,..."`. Hardcoded whitelists in extraction layers are an anti-pattern; per [search results from older versions](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/05/22/) some external write-ups already document this config key as if it exists, suggesting it was either planned or removed.
2. **Add a title-resolver fallback** so `[[Some Title]]` resolves to the slug of the page whose `title` (or `frontmatter.aliases[]`) matches. This is the Obsidian-compatibility move. Could be opt-in via a `link_extraction.resolve_by_title=true` config.
3. **Emit a warning** when extract finishes with `created: 0` despite the source having `count(*) > 0` pages — silent failure here is the worst part.

### Operator workaround (today, no upstream changes)

Pre-process the markdown during sync: rewrite all `[[Title]]` and `[[Path/Title]]` references to `[[canonical-slug/path]]` form before ingest. ~50-line Python pre-commit hook in the sync repo. Brittle and duplicates the title→slug logic, but it's the only path forward right now for Obsidian/Notion-export users.

### Environment

- gbrain v0.41.10.1, commit `d036a97`
- 321-page corpus (Notion export → Gitea → gbrain sources via path-based ingestion)
- Backend: Postgres
title: extract links: hardcoded DIR_PATTERN whitelist drops all Obsidian/Notion-style wikilinks silently (created: 0 from N pages, no warning)

body:
### Summary

`gbrain dream` (and `gbrain dream --dry-run`) consistently fails with `No database connection: connect() has not been called` on every phase that needs the brain engine, even though the serve process at the same `DATABASE_URL` works fine. `lint` and `backlinks` (which presumably use a different connect path) complete successfully in the same invocation.

### Environment

- gbrain v0.41.10.1, commit `d036a97`
- Backend: Postgres (Supabase self-hosted; not PGLite)
- `gbrain init --url <postgres-url>` ran cleanly on container first-boot; `serve --http --port 3131` works, OAuth flow works, `query`/`search`/`extract links` all hit the DB normally
- `/var/lib/gbrain/.gbrain/config.json` contains `engine` + `database_url` fields (6 lines total)

### Repro

```bash
docker exec gbrain gbrain dream --dry-run
```

Output (abridged):

```
  ! lint        523 issue(s) found (dry-run, no writes)
  ✓ backlinks   no missing back-links found
  ✗ sync        sync phase failed
      [InternalError/UNKNOWN] No database connection: connect() has not been called. Fix: Run gbrain init --supabase or gbrain init --url <connection_string>
  ✗ synthesize  synthesize phase failed
      [InternalError/SYNTH_PHASE_FAIL] No database connection: connect() has not been called.
  ✗ extract_facts ...
  ✗ patterns ...
  ✗ recompute_emotional_weight ...
  ✗ consolidate ...
  ✗ propose_takes ...
  ✗ grade_takes ...
  ✗ calibration_profile ...
  ✗ embed ...
  ✗ orphans ...
  ✗ schema-suggest ...
```

The same failure occurs with `HOME=/var/lib/gbrain` explicitly passed via `docker exec -e HOME=...`. The cycle lock IS acquired (a row appears in `gbrain_cycle_locks`), so SOME DB connection is being established — just not the one each phase needs.

### Impact

Dream cycle (the documented backbone of "consolidate → propose_takes → grade_takes → calibration_profile") is unusable on Postgres backends. Brains running on the Postgres engine can't populate the `facts` table, `recall` returns empty, autopilot doesn't help (presumably hits the same path).

### Guess at root cause

The phase runner appears to construct its own brain-engine instance without inheriting the connection pool the parent `dream` process used to acquire the cycle lock. Looks like a DI gap — the lock-subsystem connect happens, the per-phase engine instances don't reuse it and don't reconnect from config.

### Workaround used

Switched to running individual extract-and-embed steps via host cron (`gbrain extract links --source db && gbrain embed --stale`) until this lands. Drops fact consolidation / take grading entirely.

### Related

- The lock row in `gbrain_cycle_locks` has a 30-min TTL but stays held on phase failure (acquired by failed dream-cycle, not released on the engine-side crash). Workaround: `DELETE FROM gbrain_cycle_locks WHERE id='gbrain-cycle';` as superuser. Possibly worth a separate `gbrain dream --abort` CLI.
title: dream: phases fail with "No database connection" on Postgres backend (lint+backlinks succeed in same run)

body:
## Description

The minion worker subprocess (`gbrain jobs work`) spawned by `gbrain autopilot` crashes repeatedly because `engine.connect()` has not been called before the worker's main loop attempts to access the database.

This prevents ALL autopilot cycles from completing, and causes data loss during the extract phase.

## Environment

- **gbrain version**: 0.41.14.0
- **Engine**: Postgres
- **Config**: `agent.use_gateway_loop=true`, `chat_model: deepseek:deepseek-v4-flash`
- **OS**: Linux (WSL2 Ubuntu)

## Symptoms

### 1. Worker crash loop (51 crashes recorded)

```
Promotion error: No database connection: connect() has not been called. Fix: Run gbrain init --supabase or gbrain init --url <connection_string>
[autopilot] worker exited code=1 signal=null after 1409ms, crashCount=4, cause=runtime_error
[autopilot] crash backoff 8111ms (crashCount=4)
```

Error counts:
- **51** worker crashes
- **265** "Promotion error" in `promoteDelayed()`
- **446** total "No database connection" errors
- **52** batch link row losses during extraction

### 2. Data loss during extract.links_fs

```
[extract.links_fs] 60/60 (100%)
[extract.links_fs] connection blip, retrying 26 rows in 500ms (No database connection: connect() has not been called. Fix: Run gbrain init --supabase or gbrain init --url <connection_string>)
  batch error (26 link rows lost): No database connection: connect() has not been called. Fix: Run gbrain init --supabase or gbrain init --url <connection_string>
[extract.links_fs] 60/60 (100%) done
Links: created 0 from 60 pages
```

### 3. Unhandled rejection during conversation_facts_backfill

```
[cycle.conversation_facts_backfill] start
[unhandledRejection] GBrainError: No database connection: connect() has not been called. Fix: Run gbrain init --supabase or gbrain init --url <connection_string>
    at getConnection (/.../gbrain/src/core/db.ts:153:15)
    at transaction (/.../gbrain/src/core/postgres-engine.ts:764:34)
    at failJob (/.../gbrain/src/core/minions/queue.ts:855:24)
    at executeJob (/.../gbrain/src/core/minions/worker.ts:831:39)
    at processTicksAndRejections (native:7:39)
```

### 4. All sources never complete a full cycle

```
[FAIL] cycle_freshness: Source 'aevum' has never completed a full cycle;
Source 'agent-arch' has never completed a full cycle; ... (all 9 sources)
```

Brain score permanently stuck at 51/100 because link extraction (3/25), timeline extraction (2/15), and orphan resolution (1/15) cannot complete.

## Root Cause Analysis

The main `gbrain` CLI process correctly calls `createEngine()` → `connectWithRetry()` at startup (`src/cli.ts:1687-1691`). However, the minion worker is spawned as a **separate** `bun gbrain jobs work` subprocess by the supervisor (`src/core/minions/supervisor.ts`). This child process also goes through the full CLI initialization and should call `connectWithRetry()`, but something in the worker's startup path causes `connect()` to not be reliably established before `promoteDelayed()` or `executeJob()` attempts DB access.

The worker's `queue.promoteDelayed()` runs in the main loop before any job is claimed (`src/core/minions/worker.ts:434`), and if the engine's connection pool isn't ready at that point, the error is caught but the worker then crashes with an unhandled rejection when `executeJob()` later tries to call `getConnection()`.

## Contrast: Main process works fine

The parent autopilot process and direct `gbrain dream` invocations work correctly — DB access succeeds. Only the minion worker child process experiences this.

## Suggested Fix Direction

The worker's main loop should verify that `engine.connect()` has been called before entering the `promoteDelayed()` / claim / execute cycle. Alternatively, the connection should be lazily established on first use rather than requiring an explicit `connect()` call.

A workaround might be to pass `GBRAIN_DATABASE_URL` as an environment variable so the worker can self-initialize without depending on parent state.

## Related

- PR #1488 (gateway tool schema fix for non-Anthropic providers) — submitted separately
- The subagent functionality is also affected because subagent phases (consolidate, synthesize) run inside the worker process
title: autopilot: minion worker crashes with "No database connection: connect() has not been called"

body:
### Summary

The "Register Agent" form in the admin dashboard at `:3131/admin` exposes only three fields (Agent Name, Scopes, Token Lifetime), but `oauth_clients` has ~15 columns. For brains with anything other than a single `default` source, this silently produces clients that **cannot see any content** unless the operator knows to run a SQL `UPDATE` or recreate the client via CLI.

### Repro

1. Install gbrain (any v0.34.1+). Add federated sources, e.g.:
   ```bash
   gbrain sources add notion-active --path /data/repos/notion-active --federated
   gbrain sources add notion-archive --path /data/repos/notion-archive --federated
   gbrain sync --source notion-active --no-pull
   gbrain sync --source notion-archive --no-pull
   ```
2. Open the admin dashboard → click `+ OAuth Client` → fill in `Name: claude-test`, check `read` + `write`, leave token lifetime default → Register.
3. Use the returned `client_id`/`client_secret` to do `client_credentials` exchange → token returns scope `read write` ✓.
4. Call `tools/list` → returns 76 tools ✓.
5. Call `tools/call` `list_pages` (no `source_id`) → returns `[]`.
6. Call `query` with any string → `[]`.
7. Even passing `source_id: "__all__"` → still `[]`.

### Root cause

`src/core/oauth-provider.ts` `registerClient` (the handler behind `POST /admin/api/register-client`) hardcodes the row:

```ts
INSERT INTO oauth_clients (..., source_id, federated_read)
VALUES (..., 'default', ARRAY['default'])
```

The form's body has no path to override this. The brain has content in `notion-active` and `notion-archive` but the new client's `federated_read = {default}` filters all rows out.

### Why this matters

Per `docs/tutorials/company-brain.md`, multi-source / multi-user setups are an upstream-supported pattern. The tutorial **does** show how to register clients correctly via `gbrain auth register-client --source X --federated-read Y,Z`. But the admin UI dashboard appears to be the obvious entry point for new operators, and it silently steers them into a broken state with no warning. The form succeeds, the token exchanges fine, scope reports `read write` — and reads still return `[]`. Debugging takes hours.

### Adjacent gap

There is no `gbrain auth update-client` / `rescope` subcommand. The comment in `src/core/oauth-provider.ts` even says:

> "Operators who need narrower / wider scope rescope via the CLI later."

— but the rescope CLI isn't shipped. Currently the only path to fix an already-registered client is direct `UPDATE oauth_clients SET federated_read = ...` against Postgres. The same gap is acknowledged in `TODOS.md` for the threading work in 14 other read-side ops.

### Proposed fix

Either:
1. **Add the missing fields to the Register Agent form**: `source_id` (dropdown of known sources), `federated_read` (multi-select of known sources), and optionally the `bound_*` knobs.
2. **Add a Rescope/Edit button + dialog on the agent row** in the Agents page, since post-create updates are also needed when sources are added.
3. At minimum, **warn in the form**: "Multi-source brain detected — clients created here will default to the `default` source. Use `gbrain auth register-client --source X --federated-read Y,Z` for federated scoping." Cheap to ship, gets operators to the documented path.

Related: #1036 (same class of bug — `redirect_uris` field missing from the same form).

### Workaround for operators reading this

```bash
docker exec gbrain gbrain auth register-client "<name>" \
  --grant-types client_credentials \
  --scopes "read write" \
  --source <primary-source-for-writes> \
  --federated-read <source1>,<source2>,<...>
```

— or after-the-fact, the SQL one-liner:

```sql
UPDATE oauth_clients
SET federated_read = ARRAY['source1','source2','default'],
    source_id      = 'source1'
WHERE client_id = 'gbrain_cl_<...>';
```

The `federated_read` column is JOINed live from `oauth_clients` on every auth lookup (see `oauth-provider.ts` `validateToken`), so the SQL takes effect immediately on the existing access token — no client rotation needed.

### Environment

- gbrain v0.41.10.1 (commit d036a97)
- engine: postgres (Supabase)
- transport: HTTP MCP
- multi-source brain with 2 federated sources
title: admin: "Register Agent" form does not expose source_id / federated_read — multi-source clients silently broken

body:
## Bug description

When `agent.use_gateway_loop=true` and the subagent model is a non-Anthropic
provider (e.g. DeepSeek, OpenRouter, any OpenAI-compatible recipe), the gateway
tool loop fails with:

```
[chat(deepseek:deepseek-v4-flash)] schema is not a function. 
(In 'schema()', 'schema' is an instance of Object)
```

## Root cause

In `src/core/ai/gateway.ts`, the `chat()` function passes tool schemas as raw
JSON Schema objects under the `inputSchema` key to the AI SDK's `generateText()`:

```typescript
// gateway.ts ~line 2360
const tools = (opts.tools ?? []).reduce((acc, t) => {
    acc[t.name] = {
      description: t.description,
      inputSchema: { jsonSchema: t.inputSchema } as any,
    };
    return acc;
  }, {} as Record<string, any>);
```

The Vercel AI SDK expects tools to use the `parameters` key with a
`jsonSchema()`-wrapped value (the `jsonSchema` helper from the `ai` package).
The `inputSchema` key is silently ignored for openai-compatible providers.

When the SDK resolves tool definitions, it attempts to call `.schema()` on
whatever lands under `parameters` — which is `undefined` since that key is
never set. This falls through to the `inputSchema` value (a plain Object),
and the `.schema()` call fails.

## Scope of impact

This bug affects **all non-Anthropic providers** using the gateway tool loop
path (`agent.use_gateway_loop=true`). The default Anthropic-direct subagent
path is unaffected because it bypasses `gateway.chat()` entirely.

**Affected versions:** all releases that include `agent.use_gateway_loop`
support — introduced in the v0.38 series and present through current HEAD
(v0.41.14.0).

## Reproduction

Prerequisites: a configured non-Anthropic chat model with API key set.

```bash
gbrain config set agent.use_gateway_loop true
# Create a transcript file >2000 chars
gbrain dream --phase synthesize --input <transcript>
```

Result: the significance judge phase completes, but the subagent job dies
with `schema is not a function`.

## Fix

Import `jsonSchema` from the `ai` package and use the correct `parameters`
key with `jsonSchema()` wrapping:

```diff
-import { embed as aiEmbed, embedMany, generateObject, generateText } from 'ai';
+import { embed as aiEmbed, embedMany, generateObject, generateText, jsonSchema } from 'ai';

-      inputSchema: { jsonSchema: t.inputSchema } as any,
+      parameters: jsonSchema(t.inputSchema),
```

## Additional context

Discovered while running a Hermes multi-agent cluster with DeepSeek as the
primary LLM. A fix branch is available at
`justemu/gbrain:fix/gateway-tool-json-schema` for reference.
title: gateway.chat(): incorrect tool schema format breaks non-Anthropic providers (agent.use_gateway_loop)

body:
**What happened?**

在 Windows 上执行 `bun install` 时，postinstall 脚本因使用了 Unix shell 重定向语法（`1>&2`、`>/dev/null`、`command -v`）导致 Bun 的内置脚本解析器解析失败，安装报错退出。

**What did you expect?**

`bun install` 应正常完成安装，postinstall 脚本在检测到环境不满足条件时应优雅跳过，而非报错退出。

**Steps to reproduce**
1. 在 Windows 11 环境下 `git clone` gbrain 仓库
2. 执行 `bun install`
3. 观察终端输出：postinstall 阶段报错，`exit code 1`

**Environment**
- gbrain version: 0.41.14.0
- OS: Windows 11 Home China 10.0.26200
- Bun version: 1.3.14
- Database: N/A（`bun install` 阶段，尚未配置数据库）

**`gbrain doctor --json` output**
```
N/A — 安装未完成，gbrain 二进制尚未可用
```

**Additional context**

失败行位于 `package.json` 的 postinstall 脚本：

```json
"postinstall": "command -v gbrain >/dev/null 2>&1 && gbrain apply-migrations --yes --non-interactive || echo '...' 1>&2"
```

三个不兼容 Windows 的语法点：

- `command -v` — POSIX shell 命令，Bun 在 Windows 上的内置解析器不支持
- `>/dev/null 2>&1` — `/dev/null` 在 Windows 上不存在，`2>&1` 被 Bun 解析器拒绝
- `1>&2` — 触发错误 `expected a command or assignment but got: "Redirect"`

临时修复：将 postinstall 替换为 `node -e "try{...}catch(e){...}"` 跨平台写法后，`bun install` 可正常完成。
title: Windows: bun install fails due to Unix shell redirect syntax in postinstall script

body:
## Summary

When `gbrain query` / `gbrain search` is run without `--source` and without
`GBRAIN_SOURCE` set, the CLI scopes to the source named literally `default`
(which holds whatever the user captured with bare `gbrain put` — typically a
small set of notes/learnings) and silently ignores every other source in
the brain.

This makes the brain feel "broken at search" for any user whose data lives
in named sources (federated dirs, code repos, m365, imessage, calendar, etc.) —
queries return zero or near-zero hits because the bulk of the corpus is invisible.

## Repro

Brain with multiple sources:

```
$ gbrain sources list
SOURCES
  default               federated       3394 pages  never synced
  browser               federated      69793 pages
  calendar              federated       6410 pages
  contacts              federated        763 pages
  imessage              federated     169396 pages
  m365                  federated     179947 pages
  gstack-code-*         federated       (~5K total)
```

Query for content that exists only in m365:

```
$ gbrain query "Garry Tan" --limit 10
[0.78] transcripts/claude-code/.../...  (in 'default')
[0.64] transcripts/claude-code/.../...  (in 'default')
[0.64] transcripts/claude-code/.../...  (in 'default')
(nothing from m365 — but m365 has thousands of matching pages)
```

Now scope explicitly:

```
$ gbrain query "Garry Tan" --source m365 --limit 10
[0.40] mail/2026/05/20260524t213050z-...  ← hit
[0.40] mail/2026/05/20260522t125202z-...  ← hit
[0.40] mail/2026/05/20260517t102916z-...  ← hit
(5 hits, all relevant)
```

The same query against `--source __all__` does work (cross-source pipeline runs
correctly), so the routing logic is fine — it's just that the silent default
is `default` instead of `__all__`.

## Expected behavior (suggestion)

For interactive queries, `OperationContext.sourceId` should default to `__all__`
(cross-source) when no explicit scope is set. The source named `default` is a
catch-all bucket; treating it as the fallback search scope means users with
named sources get a surprising empty-result experience.

Alternatives if `__all__`-by-default feels too broad:

1. Add a global config setting `query.default_source_scope` (values: `__all__`,
   `default`, or a named source) that users can flip once.
2. Warn / hint in CLI output when a bare query returns 0 hits but the brain has
   sources beyond `default`:
   `  Hint: this brain has 7 sources; you searched only 'default'. Try --source __all__.`
3. Make the help text for `--source-id` explicit that "no flag = source named 'default', not cross-source" (current wording is ambiguous).

## Environment

- gbrain v0.41.0.0
- Postgres engine
- Brain: 440K+ pages across 7+ named federated sources

## Related

- #1167 (closed in v0.37.7.0) — fixed `--source` on the WRITE path; this issue
  is the analogous polish on the READ path's default fallback.
- Companion CLAUDE.md note re-confirmed on v0.37.9.0 that `code-def`/`code-refs`/
  `code-callers`/`code-callees` are still source-flag no-ops on the read path —
  separate sub-issue worth tracking.

## Workaround

Until this lands, users can `export GBRAIN_SOURCE=__all__` in their shell
init. Explicit `--source <name>` still overrides for narrow scopes.
title: `gbrain query` silently defaults to source='default' instead of cross-source — invisible miss when content lives in named sources

body:
## Bug Description

PGLite WASM runtime aborts immediately on Linux during initialization in gbrain v0.41.14.0.

## Environment

- Linux 6.8.0-111-generic (x64)
- Node.js v24.14.0
- Bun 2.x
- gbrain version: 0.41.14.0 (upgraded from 0.40.8.0)
- PGlite bundled version: v0.4.3

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `gbrain sync` or any command that triggers the engine (e.g., `gbrain doctor`)
2. PGLite WASM initialization aborts immediately

## Expected Behavior

PGLite should initialize successfully and connect to the local database at `~/.gbrain/brain.pglite`.

## Actual Behavior

```
PGLite WASM abort — the WASM runtime calls abort() during initialization.
```

The error occurs at the WASM level, not in TypeScript/JavaScript code. This happens before any DB operations.

## Relevant Logs

```
Schema version 92 → 97 (5 migration(s) pending)
  [93] minions_v0_41_audit_and_budget...
  [93] ✓ minions_v0_41_audit_and_budget
  ... (migrations apply fine)
  [doctor.db_checks] start
  [doctor.db_checks] connection
  [doctor.db_checks] pgvector
  [doctor.db_checks] pgbouncer_prepare
  ...
  [doctor.db_checks] embedding_provider
  [doctor.db_checks] embedding_env_override
  [OK] embedding_provider: Skipped (no provider credentials). Model: zeroentropyai:zembed-1.
  [WARN] ze_embedding_health: embedding_model="zeroentropyai:zembed-1" but ZEROENTROPY_API_KEY is not set.
  (and then eventually PGLite WASM aborts)
```

Note: `gbrain sync` reports "No changes — brain is up to date" because the sync logic uses a workaround script that bypasses the CLI machinery. The abort occurs when using commands that directly initialize the PGLite engine.

## Additional Context

- The database at `~/.gbrain/brain.pglite` exists and is intact (data persists from v0.40.8.0)
- This appears to be a Linux-specific issue with the PGLite WASM build
- The macOS 26.3 workaround (setting certain environment flags) does not apply here
- PGlite v0.4.3 WASM initialization fails on this Linux environment
- The local data is accessible through the workaround but direct PGLite engine operations fail

## Possible Cause

The PGLite WASM binary bundled with gbrain v0.41.14.0 may have compatibility issues with certain Linux kernel/glibc combinations, or the WASM memory settings may need adjustment for Linux environments.

## Severity

High — prevents core functionality (embedding, search, cycle) on Linux systems.

---
Reported by: OpenClaw agent (Hansen1018's gbrain installation)
title: PGLite WASM abort on Linux (v0.41.14.0)

body:
**Title:** `gbrain config set eval.capture true` persists to disk but is not honored at runtime — capture only fires via `GBRAIN_CONTRIBUTOR_MODE=1`

**Version:** v0.41.14.0 (master @ `32f8be96`, Postgres engine)

**Symptom:**

```bash
$ gbrain config set eval.capture true
Set eval.capture = true

$ gbrain config get eval.capture
true

$ gbrain query "test capture" --limit 5 --detail low
# (query runs, returns results)

$ psql -c 'SELECT count(*) FROM eval_candidates;'
 count
-------
     0      # → no rows captured

$ psql -c 'SELECT count(*) FROM eval_capture_failures;'
 count
-------
     0      # → no failures logged either — capture not even attempted
```

Now with explicit env var:

```bash
$ GBRAIN_CONTRIBUTOR_MODE=1 gbrain query "test capture via env var" --limit 5 --detail low
# (same query, same brain, same flags)

$ psql -c 'SELECT count(*) FROM eval_candidates;'
 count
-------
     1      # → row captured
```

**Root cause (suspected):**

`src/core/eval-capture.ts:196-199` gate:

```typescript
export function isEvalCaptureEnabled(config: GBrainConfig | null | undefined): boolean {
  if (config?.eval?.capture === true) return true;
  if (config?.eval?.capture === false) return false;
  return process.env.GBRAIN_CONTRIBUTOR_MODE === '1';
}
```

`gbrain config set eval.capture true` writes the value to disk (verified — `gbrain config get eval.capture` returns `true`). But the runtime `config` object passed to `isEvalCaptureEnabled` does NOT contain `config.eval.capture`. So the dotted-key set lands in storage but the parsed `GBrainConfig` shape doesn't expose it under the expected nested path, and the gate falls through to the env-var fallback.

Likely a `loadConfig` / `parseConfig` issue that doesn't promote `eval.capture` dotted-key into `config.eval.capture` nested.

**Inspected:**
- `/root/.gbrain/config.json` contains only `{engine, embedding_model, embedding_dimensions, database_url, chat_model, chat_fallback_chain}` — no `eval` key visible there. The `config set eval.capture true` may be writing to a separate preferences file or a Postgres `gbrain_config` table that the runtime doesn't merge.

**Impact:**

Operators following the canonical eval-capture docs / CHANGELOG flow:
```
gbrain config set eval.capture true
# run queries
gbrain eval export --tool query > baseline.ndjson
```
…get an empty NDJSON and a silent failure. The eval-loop documented in v0.41.1 CHANGELOG (`gbrain bench publish` + `gbrain eval gate`) cannot be used without first discovering the env-var workaround.

**Workaround applied:**

Added systemd drop-in `Environment=GBRAIN_CONTRIBUTOR_MODE=1` to `gbrain-mcp.service` and `gbrain-worker.service`. Capture now fires for MCP traffic + CLI invocations that inherit the env. Tracked as local config in LOCAL_PATCHES.md.

**Suggested fix:**

Either (a) ensure `gbrain config set eval.capture true` writes a nested-shape entry that `loadConfig` materializes into `GBrainConfig.eval.capture`, or (b) document that `eval.capture` is env-var-only and reject `config set eval.capture` with a helpful error pointing at the env var.

(Filed by Mr-B-1 — sibling issues #1452-1455, #1474 from the same v0.40.8 → v0.41.14 upgrade audit.)
title: gbrain config set eval.capture true persists but is not honored at runtime (capture silently no-ops without GBRAIN_CONTRIBUTOR_MODE=1)

body:
## Summary
`gbrain query` (hybrid) returns the **same** top documents with **identical scores** for completely unrelated queries — including nonsense input. `gbrain search` (BM25) is query-responsive and correct. The hybrid/vector path appears to ignore the query.

## Environment
- gbrain **0.41.11.1** (also reproduced on 0.35.6.0 before upgrading)
- Engine: postgres
- Embedding: `ollama:nomic-embed-text`, 768d (`gbrain doctor`: `embedding_provider ✓`, `embedding_width_consistency` OK)
- macOS / bun

## Repro
```
gbrain query "wyckoff accumulation"     # top-3 docs A,B,C @ ~0.99/0.98/0.97
gbrain query "banana recipe quantum"    # IDENTICAL top-3 A,B,C @ IDENTICAL scores
gbrain query "..." --no-expand          # still identical
gbrain search "wyckoff accumulation"    # correct, query-responsive
gbrain search "banana recipe quantum"   # different/correct results
```
Different queries → identical `query` results **and identical cosine scores** ⇒ the query vector used in the hybrid search appears fixed/independent of the input.

## Ruled out (investigated)
- **Embedding model**: ollama `nomic-embed-text` returns distinct vectors for distinct inputs (cosine ≈0.47 between the two example queries, via both `/api/embeddings` and `/v1/embeddings`, with and without `input_type`).
- **Semantic query cache**: `gbrain cache clear --yes`, then fresh (cache-miss) queries are still identical.
- **Query expansion**: `--no-expand` still identical.
- **BM25 (`search`)**: correct and query-responsive — data + BM25 path are fine.

## Hypothesis
The query-side embedding (`embedQuery` → gateway `embed([text], {inputType:'query'})`) or its use in `core/search/hybrid.ts` may produce/use a fixed query vector. On every query this warning prints: `[ai.gateway] recipe "google" declares an embedding touchpoint without max_batch_tokens` — possibly related to query-embedding routing, even though the configured embedding model is `ollama:nomic-embed-text`.

Happy to provide more detail or test a patch.
title: hybrid `query` returns identical results regardless of input (vector path); `search` (BM25) correct
